prompt: The Null Object pattern still causes NullPointerException. What is the bug?
choices: NullLogger must extend ConsoleLogger to be used as a Logger
getLogger(false) returns null instead of a NullLogger instance; calling log.log() on null throws NullPointerException
The Null Object pattern requires an abstract class, not an interface
log.log() must be wrapped in a null check at the call site
explanation: The whole purpose of the Null Object pattern is to avoid null returns and the null checks they require. getLogger(false) returns null, which defeats the pattern. Fix: return new NullLogger() instead of null. The caller can then call log.log() safely without a null check.
